channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: A presidential first: Trump at the Super Bowl, latest chapter in a complicated legacy with football
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — As a student, Donald Trump played high school football. As a business baron, he owned a team in an upstart rival to the NFL and then sued the established league. As president, he denigrated pros who took a knee during the national anthem as part of a social justice movement.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he is serious about Canada becoming 51st state in Super Bowl interview
item.description: President Donald Trump said he is serious about wanting Canada to become the 51st state in an interview that aired Sunday during the Super Bowl preshow.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says he will announce 25% steel and aluminum tariffs Monday, and more import duties are coming
item.description: President Donald Trump says he'll announce on Monday that the United States will impose 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports, including from Canada and Mexico, as well as other import duties later in the week.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Rugaber, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Richard Gere calls Trump a 'bully and a thug' at awards show
item.description: Hollywood star Richard Gere called President Trump a “bully” and a “thug” in his acceptance speech at the Spanish Academy’s Goya Awards on Saturday night. While accepting his honorary lifetime achievement award, Gere warned of the “slippery slope” toward authoritarianism in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE cancels funding for Fauci museum exhibit
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency announced on social media that the Department of Health and Human services was canceling a planned Anthony Fauci museum exhibit.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance and Musk question the authority of the courts as Trump's agenda faces legal pushback
item.description: Top Trump administration officials are openly questioning the judiciary’s authority to serve as a check on executive power as the new president’s sweeping agenda faces growing pushback from the courts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Fed up GOP congressman dispels liberal 'lies' against DOGE in letter to constituents
item.description: Republican Virginia Rep. Ben Cline sent a letter to constituents dispelling liberal "lies" surrounding Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration orders consumer protection agency to stop work, closes building
item.description: The Trump administration has ordered the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to stop nearly all its work, effectively shutting down the agency that was created to protect consumers after the 2008 financial crisis and subprime mortgage-lending scandal.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Rugaber, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DOGE siege of USAID dominated week in politics
item.description: USAID operations have effectively been put on hold, less than three weeks after Trump began his second term in office.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Spending bill talks bog down after Trump's efforts to slash government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Before President Donald Trump and Republicans in Congress can enact much of their legislative agenda, they have to deal with some unfinished business — completing work on the current budget year's spending bills. It's a task that by all accounts is not going well.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he's firing Kennedy Center board of trustees members and naming himself chairman
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump says he is firing members of the board of trustees for the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts and naming himself chairman.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk perched behind Trump's desk in new TIME Magazine cover
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk is seen perched behind President Donald Trump's desk in the Oval Office in their latest cover.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Plan to break up Nevada's biggest agency could bring lower health care costs
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo is no fan of big government, and a plan he announced in January's State of the State address takes aim at Nevada's biggest agency, the Department of Health and Human Services.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump signs order to establish White House Faith Office
item.description: Trump directed the office to identify ways to reduce burdens on the free exercise of religion and direct all federal agencies to designate a “faith liaison” within 90 days.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota joins lawsuit against Trump’s move to restrict gender-affirming care
item.description: Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison, along with attorneys general from Washington and Oregon and three unnamed doctors, filed a federal lawsuit Friday calling for a halt on a presidential executive order that would end federal funding to medical institutions that provide gender-affirming care.
item.dc:creator: Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: North Carolina judge's ruling retains close court election result, but more legal steps are expected
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — A North Carolina trial judge on Friday upheld decisions by election officials to reject protests by the trailing candidate in a very close state Supreme Court election who wants tens of thousands of contested ballots removed from the race tallies.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks USAID purge, evacuations from host countries
item.description: A federal judge late Friday temporarily blocked the Trump administration’s plan to place thousands of U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) employees on leave at midnight.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Musk says he'll bring back DOGE staffer under fire for racist posts
item.description: Elon Musk said Friday he will rehire a former Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffer who resigned after several of the aide's racist social media posts were exposed. "He will be brought back," Musk posted on X, the social media platform he purchased in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, NewsNation

item.title: Judges curb Trump’s sweeping executive actions
item.description: Federal judges are curbing President Trump’s sweeping directives to reshape the government, issuing a flurry of rulings blocking his agenda from charging forward.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Tribal colleges and universities react to executive order rescinding support efforts for Indigenous education
item.description: The Trump administration’s sweeping orders could force some tribal colleges and universities to shut down, school leaders said.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: 12 states to sue over DOGE access to payment systems containing personal data
item.description: “As the richest man in the world, Elon Musk is not used to being told ‘no,’ but in our country, no one is above the law," a statement from the Democratic attorneys general reads.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NCAA changes transgender policy to limit women's competition to athletes assigned female at birth
item.description: The NCAA changed its participation policy for transgender athletes on Thursday, limiting competition in women's sports to athletes assigned female at birth only.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Las Vegas under partial hiring freeze as city readies for Badlands deal
item.description: Las Vegas expects a settlement soon in litigation over the Badlands golf course, and has already begun to freeze hiring to save money, according to city officials.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Israel defense minister tells army to prepare to relocate Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: He says the military should prepare exit options by land, sea and air so that anyone who wishes to can leave for "any country willing to accept them.
item.dc:creator: Jerome Socolovsky and Ryland Barton, MPR News

item.title: FBI agents, prosecutors fear retribution from Jan. 6 rioters pardoned by Trump
item.description: Officials involved in Jan. 6 prosecutions say the Trump administration isn't protecting them from threats. "We don't think they'll care — unless and until one of us gets killed," an official told NPR.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota Supreme Court hears arguments on House conflict despite GOP, DFL agreement
item.description: The Minnesota Supreme Court heard oral arguments Thursday morning in a case involving a standoff in the Minnesota House that has dragged on for more than three weeks, though GOP and DFL leaders had reached an agreement Wednesday night to resolve the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Renters' rights and 13 more bills to watch at the 2025 Nevada Legislature
item.description: Lawmakers are busy in Carson City drawing up new rules to live by, including tougher penalties for committing crimes, protections for transgender Nevadans and a variety of laws related to housing and renters' rights.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nassau police to help ICE detain undocumented immigrants accused of crimes
item.description: NASSAU COUNTY, N.Y. (PIX11) – The Nassau County Police Department will be directly participating in ICE enforcement to detain undocumented immigrants accused of crimes, County Executive Bruce Blakeman announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Local congressman reacts to inspectors general dismissed by President Trump
item.description: In the flurry of Trump's executive orders covering immigration raids, federal firings, funding freezes and tariff negotiations, the dismissing of 17 inspector generals has gotten little notice, but according to San Diego Congressman Mike Levin, this is a bigger issue than most understand.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5

item.title: Trump, Mexican president agree to delay tariffs for one month
item.description: President Trump and Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said they have agreed to delay tariffs by at least one month, following a call between the two leaders Monday. The 25 percent tariffs on Mexico were to be implemented Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USAID leaders escorted out of building after blocking DOGE access to secure systems
item.description: Officials said USAID members were eventually unsuccessful and DOGE teams were able to gain access to some of the agency’s classified information, including intelligence reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump kept his tariffs pledge. Is he ready for the fallout? Are Americans? What to know
item.description: The Budget Lab at Yale University estimates Trump’s tariffs would cost the average American household $1,000 to $1,200 in annual purchasing power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
